title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Dr. Temple defends his decision to take Hester Lincoln away from Paul Roman, whom he describes as a treacherous scoundrel and a leader of the "sun cult." Temple claims he intervened because Hester is a confused teenager and notes that he had a disagreement with Roman earlier that morning. Temple expresses a desire to marry Hester, a sentiment she meets with a long, shining gaze.

The group, including Ellery, Isam, and Professor Yadley, travels by boat back to Oyster Island. During the trip, Dr. Temple reveals his background as a retired military doctor who served in Italy and was captured by the Austrians during World War I. Despite Temple's protests, Hester insists on returning to the island, defying his attempts to control her.

Upon landing, the investigators encounter Harakat, a disheveled old man in a white robe carrying a snake-headed staff, and Paul Roman. Harakat views the investigators as pagans. Officer Vane, acting as the district attorney and chief of police, confronts Roman regarding the murder of Thomas Breda. Roman claims innocence, stating that he and Harakat have been on the island continuously, though Roman visited a village for supplies a week prior. The investigators follow Harakat toward a large, haphazardly constructed wooden hut. They observe that Roman appears to have given a warning, as Harakat's followers are already dressed and gathered in front of the structure.
